title: Negative versus positive
sub_topics: Marxist slogan ('From each according to his ability...')
1987 Constitution Article XII Section 1 (positive economic goals)
Two great commandments of Christ (love God + love neighbor)
Election mantras (Alis diyan, Tama na Sobra na Palitan na)
professorial chairs / legal scholarships / dissertation contests / educational assistance
Interactive Museum of Liberty and Prosperity
FLP second-decade focus on MBA scholarships and extreme poverty

stances.claim: Communism was appealing in theory but appalling in practice — to a dirt-poor student the Marxist slogan seemed to fulfill his longings, but the masses under communist regimes were maltreated and impoverished.

Negatives are not enough; positives are indispensable — the Bill of Rights, the Ten Commandments, and election mantras are mostly written in negative language; positive constitutional provisions and Christ's two great commandments offer the complementary affirmative vision.
stances.rhetorical_move: negative-positive structural critique across legal, religious, and political texts

stances.claim: Slogans and tweets rarely transform into fulfillment because they are not backed up by sustainable philosophies, implementable programs, and realistic timelines.
stances.rhetorical_move: philosophical-operational triad as test of substance

stances.claim: His Supreme Court tenure left him with a yearning that he had not done enough — most of his decisions were negative in language (commanding parties to stop, dismissing petitions); FLP was founded to fulfill the positive side.
stances.rhetorical_move: judicial self-assessment + transition narrative to FLP founding

stances.claim: FLP transformed liberty-safeguarding from dream to sustainable reality because its professors, scholars, dissertation winners, and educational-assistance beneficiaries will continue and expand the philosophy 'beyond my lifetime.'

signature_phrases: Communism was appealing in theory but appalling in practice
theory versus practice, dreams versus realities, negative versus positive, slogans versus philosophies
sustainable philosophies, implementable programs, and realistic timelines
a yearning throbbed deep in my heart
register_markers: When I was a pre-law student
Tagalog code-switching (Alis diyan / Tama na Sobra na Palitan na)
decision_framework_signals: applies negative-vs-positive frame to legal, religious, and political texts as unified diagnostic lens
uses philosophy-program-timeline triad as test of whether stated ideals are operationalizable
frames generational continuation (scholars/professors who outlive the founder) as the operative success metric for philanthropic institutions
notable_anecdotes: Pre-law political science professor repeatedly challenging him to refute the Marxist 'from each according to his ability' slogan, which the dirt-poor young Panganiban could not easily refute

Being invited after retirement by Philippine tycoons (most of whom he had not previously met) to join their boards, leading to discovery of their philanthropy and corporate-social-responsibility yearnings
FLP receiving over P164 million in donations from partners, friends, and his family across its first decade
